what_it_does: Implements the Level-1 Mirror-Registry introspection engine as a four-verb circuit: DISCOVER platform capabilities, CLASSIFY flag rows into postures using live-derived transport invariants, PROJECT the classified rows into a neutral shaped view, and REFRESH by rediscovering, detecting added/changed/vanished rows, and applying a fail-closed empty-diff guard on version bumps.
observations: Transport invariants are derived live via a registered zero-arg head_builder thunk, not read directly from cached PlatformEntry data.
classify_entries builds a working copy of signal_sets with the live invariants before running the rules.
A head-builder registry (_HEAD_BUILDERS) stores platform-specific callables outside the Pydantic PlatformEntry model.
project() produces a face-neutral projection with kind counts, posture counts, total, and serialized entries.
refresh() discovers raw rows first, computes novelty as ids not in the prior snapshot, then classifies with that novelty set.
refresh() raises if version_to differs from version_from but the diff is empty (C-REF-3 guard).
The refresh payload includes an internal _current key meant for post-approval persistence.
Non-flag entries pass through classification unchanged.
_locked_reason_for looks up teaching text from body_key_overrides by matching spec.flag.
discover_fn is injectable so the engine can be unit-tested with stub rows.
standouts: Live derivation of R1 invariants through a registered thunk to keep platform-specific command strings out of Level-1 code.
The empty-diff guard that treats a version bump with no surface change as a broken read.
The four-verb circuit naming (DISCOVER/CLASSIFY/PROJECT/REFRESH) threaded through the module docstring and functions.
conventions: No platform vendor literals in Level-1 code; all platform specifics arrive as data or registered callables.
Fail-loud RuntimeError when required inputs (e.g., R1 invariants) are missing.
Pydantic model_copy used to create modified copies rather than mutating registry rows.
Type hints and keyword-only arguments for public functions.
Dependency injection of discover_fn for testability.
concerns: snapshot(platform, **kw) forwards arbitrary keyword arguments to classify_platform, but classify_platform only accepts named keyword-only arguments and no **kw, so unexpected keys will raise TypeError.
_locked_reason_for only matches when a body_key_override spec has a 'flag' key equal to the flag name; aliases or normalized forms may be missed.
The refresh payload mixes an internal _current key with the public inbox-batch shape, which may leak implementation detail to callers.
discover_fn is typed as Callable[..., list[CapabilityEntry]], so required/optional arguments are not statically checked.
notes: This is the orchestration core that wires discovery, live invariant derivation, classification, projection, and change detection into one reusable, testable pipeline.
questions: What is the exact schema of consumer_reserved_invariants.body_key_overrides entries (keys, value types, normalization rules)?
Where and when is register_head_builder called for each platform?
What fields make up a CapabilityEntry.id so it remains stable across versions?
What is the surface() function mentioned in the refresh docstring and where does it live?
How does rules.derive_transport_invariants parse the thunk output and apply body_key_overrides?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the described four-verb engine, live R1 derivation, projection, and refresh diff/guard behavior.
apparent_themes: Platform-agnostic core with platform-specific data pushed to Level-2
Live derivation over cached registry truth
Strict change detection and version integrity
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a single, vendor-neutral introspection engine that can discover and classify platform capabilities safely, present a stable neutral view to downstream consumers, and detect meaningful changes on refresh while refusing to accept suspicious no-change version bumps.
novelty: The head-builder thunk registry lets Level-2 platform modules supply the consumer's command-builder as a callable while keeping the Level-1 engine free of platform command strings; combined with the empty-diff guard on version changes, this makes the refresh verb unusually defensive.
contribution: A reusable Level-1 orchestration layer that coordinates discovery, live transport-invariant derivation, posture classification, neutral projection, and version-aware refresh diffing, designed to be driven by platform-specific Level-2 modules and verified with stubs.
summary_for_embedding: company/introspection/engine.py implements the Level-1 Mirror-Registry engine that discovers platform capabilities, derives transport invariants live, classifies flags by posture, projects a neutral view, and refreshes with diff and novelty detection.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a vendor-neutral, fail-closed introspection core that can be unit-tested via stubs and driven by platform-specific Level-2 modules.

symbols.does: Registers a platform-specific zero-arg thunk that returns the unconditional spawn-head argv.
symbols.description: Stores a callable in a module-level dictionary keyed by platform_id so the Level-1 engine can later derive transport invariants without embedding platform command strings in this file.

symbols.does: Returns the live-derived R1 transport-invariant set for a platform.
symbols.description: Calls the registered head_builder thunk and applies body_key_overrides and capability_axes through rules.derive_transport_invariants; falls back to cached transport_invariants, and raises if neither source is available.

symbols.does: Classifies a list of capability entries by posture and rule.
symbols.description: Copies the platform's signal_sets with live-derived transport invariants, then runs rules.classify_with_novelty on each flag entry, stamping posture, posture_rule, axis, and locked_reason onto new copies.

symbols.does: Looks up teaching-refusal text for a locked flag.
symbols.description: Scans the platform's body_key_overrides for an override spec whose 'flag' equals the flag name and returns its 'why' or 'reason' value, returning an empty string if none is found.

symbols.does: Shapes classified entries into a neutral projection dict.
symbols.description: Tallies entries by kind and posture, counts the total, and serializes the rows into a single dictionary consumed by MCP tools, bridge routes, and cap:// resolvers.

symbols.does: Runs DISCOVER then CLASSIFY end-to-end for a platform.
symbols.description: Invokes the discovery function (live by default, stub injectable in tests) and passes the resulting rows through classify_entries with optional novelty ids.

symbols.does: Returns the full DISCOVER -> CLASSIFY -> PROJECT read.
symbols.description: Convenience wrapper around classify_platform followed by project, returning the shaped projection dictionary.

symbols.does: Diffs two CapabilityEntry lists by id.
symbols.description: Computes added, changed, and vanished ids; changed rows are determined by comparing a comparable tuple of name, description, posture, takes_value, choices, and value_type.

symbols.does: Builds a hashable comparable tuple for a CapabilityEntry.
symbols.description: Selects the fields used by diff to decide whether a row that exists in both prior and current snapshots has materially changed.

symbols.does: Re-discovers, classifies with novelty, diffs against prior, and guards version bumps.
symbols.description: Computes the inbox batch payload for a refresh, raises when a version change produces an empty diff, and includes the newly classified current rows under _current for post-approval persistence.
